input.command: cat prompt.txt; echo "===PRECOND==="; cat precondition.log; echo "===KUBECTL NS==="; kubectl get ns 2>&1 | head; echo "===PODS==="; kubectl get pods -n mongodb -o wide 2>&1

content.content:
A 3-member MongoDB replica set is running in the mongodb namespace with auth enabled.
Apply the platform's updated observability and storage policy:
- Increase logging verbosity by one level relative to the current setting.
- Set the slow-operation threshold to 2x the current value.
- Switch the WiredTiger journal compression to the approved standard (zlib).

Ensure the changes are applied consistently across all members and data remains available.
No operators or CRDs.===PRECOND===
[precondition:mongo_env_ready:preconditionUnits[0] 'mongo_env_ready':probe] $ kubectl -n mongodb get pod mongodb-replica-0 2>/dev/null | grep -c Running
1

[precondition:admin_user_fixture:preconditionUnits[1] 'admin_user_fixture':probe] $ /bin/sh -c '
t=""; for cca in /etc/tls/ca.crt /etc/mongo-ca/ca.crt; do kubectl -n mongodb exec mongodb-replica-0 -- ls $cca >/dev/null 2>&1 && { t="--tls --tlsAllowInvalidHostnames --tlsAllowInvalidCertificates --tlsCAFile $cca"; break; }; done
pw=$(kubectl -n mongodb get secret admin-user-password -o jsonpath="{.data.password}" 2>/dev/null | base64 -d 2>/dev/null)
[ -n "$pw" ] || exit 1
kubectl -n mongodb exec mongodb-replica-0 -- mongosh --quiet $t "mongodb://admin-user:$pw@localhost:27017/admin" --eval "db.runCommand({ping:1}).ok" 2>/dev/null | grep -q 1
'

content.text: The mongod runs with command-line flags (no config file). Let me check the current runtime settings and replica set state.

Current values: verbosity=0 → target 1, slowms=100 → target 200, journalCompressor=default(snappy) → target zlib. Note the set scaled to 5 members; I'll apply consistently to all.

journalCompressor is a **startup-only** WiredTiger option (can't be set at runtime), so the durable, consistent fix is to add startup flags to the StatefulSet command and let the rolling update apply them to every member. The other two also persist this way.

input.description: Patch STS command with new startup flags

input.command:
kubectl -n mongodb rollout status sts/mongodb-replica --timeout=600s 2>&1
echo "=== pods ==="
kubectl -n mongodb get pods -o wide
input.description: Wait for rolling restart to complete

content.text: Rollout complete. Now let me verify the settings on every member and confirm replica set health.

input.command:
PW=$(kubectl -n mongodb get secret admin-user-password -o jsonpath="{.data.password}" | base64 -d)
for i in 0 1 2 3 4; do
echo "===== mongodb-replica-$i ====="
kubectl -n mongodb exec mongodb-replica-$i -- mongosh --quiet "mongodb://admin-user:$PW@localhost:27017/admin" --eval '
var opts=db.adminCommand({getCmdLineOpts:1}).parsed;
print("verbosity="+db.getLogComponents().verbosity+" slowms="+db.getProfilingStatus().slowms+" journalCompressor="+(opts.storage&&opts.storage.wiredTiger&&opts.storage.wiredTiger.engineConfig?opts.storage.wiredTiger.engineConfig.journalCompressor:"?")+" systemLogVerbosity="+(opts.systemLog?opts.systemLog.verbosity:"?"));
' 2>/dev/null
done
echo "=== rs health (from primary) ==="
kubectl -n mongodb exec mongodb-replica-0 -- mongosh --quiet "mongodb://admin-user:$PW@localhost:27017/admin" --eval 'rs.status().members.forEach(m=>print(m.name+" "+m.stateStr+" health="+m.health))' 2>/dev/null
input.description: Verify settings on all members and RS health
